Accepted and logged; AnyTXT is a Windows-only local-search tool not applicable to the current Mac/TrueNAS/Docker stack. No behavior change in the current release.

ChatRequest.title: ChatRequest
ChatRequest.description:
Request body for POST /chat/stream (ADR-0019 §2.2, B2-C1/C2/C3).

The server NEVER accepts provider_type / model_id (I6 / Do-NOT #4): the backend resolves
`resolve_provider_config("chat", vault_id)`. `operation` is fixed to "chat" so the same
abstraction can route ingest-vs-chat differently.

B2 additions (additive, non-breaking for existing clients):
  - ``use_web_search``: trigger a single-shot SearXNG fetch (C2, I9). [W] namespace.
  - ``retrieval_mode``: one of four frozen presets controlling k + expansion_depth (C3, I7).

token.title: Token
token.description: The Claude subscription OAuth token to store (from `claude setup-token`). Encrypted at rest via Fernet (SYNAPSE_SECRET_KEY); plaintext held in-memory only for outbound CLI injection. NEVER logged or returned. Requires SYNAPSE_SECRET_KEY configured server-side (else HTTP 400 — W7). Validated: non-empty, 20–500 chars; soft prefix check (warns, does not block).

clear.title: Clear
clear.description: true ⇒ set cli_oauth_token_encrypted = NULL (fall back to env / none). Wins over token if both are sent.

CliAuthConfigRequest.title: CliAuthConfigRequest
CliAuthConfigRequest.description:
Request body for PUT /provider/cli-auth (ADR-0043 §2.5).

Exactly one of {token, clear} should be present:
  token: str  — paste the token produced by ``claude setup-token`` (prefix: sk-ant- + oat01-)
  clear: bool — true ⇒ set vault_state.cli_oauth_token = NULL (fall back to env / none)

``clear`` wins if both are sent. An empty body (neither field) → 400.

token_configured.title: Token Configured
token_configured.description: True iff any credential is available (DB cli_oauth_token set OR any env signal: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN / CLAUDE_CODE_USE_SUBSCRIPTION). NEVER contains the token value.

token_source.title: Token Source
token_source.description: "db" | "env" | "none". "db": vault_state.cli_oauth_token is set (DB wins — ADR-0043 §2.3 tier 1). "env": no DB token; at least one env signal is present (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN / CLAUDE_CODE_USE_SUBSCRIPTION). "none": nothing configured.

auth_mode.title: Auth Mode
auth_mode.description: "api-key" | "subscription" | "unconfigured". Derived from ADR-0043 §2.3 precedence (presence-only; does NOT run injection): "api-key": env ANTHROPIC_API_KEY non-empty AND no DB token. "subscription": DB token set OR env CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN/USE_SUBSCRIPTION. "unconfigured": nothing set.

CliAuthConfigResponse.title: CliAuthConfigResponse
CliAuthConfigResponse.description:
Response model for GET/PUT /provider/cli-auth (ADR-0043 §2.5).

Posture only — NEVER returns the token value. Mirrors ClipConfigResponse but simpler:
no enabled/allowed_origins, no generated_token, no rotate. The user pastes their own
token; the server never generates one.

rotate_token.title: Rotate Token
rotate_token.description: true ⇒ generate a new high-entropy token (secrets.token_urlsafe(32)), store its PBKDF2-SHA256 hash in clip_access_token (never the raw value), return plaintext ONCE in generated_token. The plaintext is NEVER stored or returned again after this call.

clear_token.title: Clear Token
clear_token.description: true ⇒ set clip_access_token = NULL (DB token cleared; falls back to CLIP_TOKEN env bootstrap or none).

set_enabled.title: Set Enabled
set_enabled.description: Set the clip_enabled_db flag. true ⇒ DB overrides CLIP_ENABLED env with True. false ⇒ DB overrides with False (ingress disabled regardless of env). Omit to leave unchanged.

set_allowed_origins.title: Set Allowed Origins
set_allowed_origins.description: Replace the DB clip_allowed_origins_db value with this comma-separated string. Empty string "" clears the DB value (falls back to CLIP_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env). Omit to leave unchanged.

ClipConfigRequest.title: ClipConfigRequest
ClipConfigRequest.description:
Request body for PUT /clip/config (ADR-0040 §2.4).

All fields are optional; omitting a field leaves that aspect unchanged.
Mirrors McpAuthRequest (ADR-0033 §2.5).

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Resolved enabled state (DB clip_enabled_db if set, else CLIP_ENABLED env). True iff POST /clip will be accepted.

token_configured.title: Token Configured
token_configured.description: True iff a token is available (DB clip_access_token PBKDF2 hash set OR CLIP_TOKEN env set). NEVER contains the token value.

token_source.title: Token Source
token_source.description: "db" | "env" | "none" — which token source is authoritative (ADR-0040 §2.2). "db": token set via PUT /clip/config. "env": CLIP_TOKEN env bootstrap. "none": no token configured. NEVER the token value.

allowed_origins.title: Allowed Origins
allowed_origins.description: Resolved allowed-origins list (DB if set, else CLIP_ALLOWED_ORIGINS env). Loopback origins are always implicitly allowed in addition to this list.

max_body_bytes.title: Max Body Bytes
max_body_bytes.description: Maximum allowed body size for POST /clip in bytes (CLIP_MAX_BODY_BYTES env). Not runtime-settable via PUT /clip/config; change the env var.

ClipConfigResponse.title: ClipConfigResponse
ClipConfigResponse.description:
Response model for GET /clip/config (ADR-0040 §2.3).

Mirrors McpInfoResponse structure: posture-only, token value NEVER returned.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Resolved enabled state after this write.

token_configured.title: Token Configured
token_configured.description: True iff a token is available after this write (DB or env).

token_source.title: Token Source
token_source.description: "db" | "env" | "none" — authoritative token source after this write.

allowed_origins.title: Allowed Origins
allowed_origins.description: Resolved allowed-origins list after this write.

generated_token.title: Generated Token
generated_token.description: Populated ONLY when rotate_token=true — the plaintext token shown ONCE. null in all other cases. NEVER stored as recoverable. NEVER returned by subsequent GET or PUT.

ClipConfigStateResponse.title: ClipConfigStateResponse
ClipConfigStateResponse.description:
Response body for PUT /clip/config (ADR-0040 §2.4).

Always reflects post-write posture. generated_token is populated ONLY when
rotate_token=true — shown ONCE, never returned again.
NEVER contains the token value (except the one-time generated_token on rotate).

url.title: Url
url.description: Source URL of the clipped page

source.title: Source
source.description: Optional source hint for the YAML frontmatter sources[] field. Defaults to the url when omitted.

vault_id.title: Vault Id
vault_id.description: Target vault identifier (W5 / PF-MCP-VAULT-1). Defaults to the active vault when omitted. Clips to a non-active vault are rejected (400) — activate the vault first.

ClipRequest.title: ClipRequest
ClipRequest.description:
Request body for POST /clip (F11, ADR-0038).

Sent by the Chrome MV3 extension after converting the article to Markdown
via Readability + Turndown. The extension owns the conversion; the server
only validates, sanitizes, and stores.

file_path.title: File Path
file_path.description: Saved path relative to vault_root, e.g. "raw/sources/Example-Article.md"

status.title: Status
status.description: "queued" — file saved to raw/sources/; watcher ingests asynchronously.

overwritten.title: Overwritten
overwritten.description: True if a same-named file already existed and was replaced on disk

ConversationCreate.title: ConversationCreate
ConversationCreate.description: Request body for POST /conversations (ADR-0019 §2.5). vault_id defaults to settings.

ConversationResponse.title: ConversationResponse
ConversationResponse.description:
API shape for one conversations row (ADR-0019 §2.5).

`preview` (UXB-1) is a read-only derivation of the conversation's last message: its first
~80 chars with <think>…</think> and light markdown stripped. It is computed in the list
handler (bounded subquery); there is no schema change. None when the conversation has no
messages yet.

CreateProjectRequest.title: CreateProjectRequest
CreateProjectRequest.description: Body for POST /projects — create + scaffold a new vault folder.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: stub (default) — deterministic draft, no provider call; generate — full LLM generation (existing behaviour, explicit opt-in).

CreateReviewBody.title: CreateReviewBody
CreateReviewBody.description:
Optional JSON body for POST /review/queue/{id}/create (and /approve alias).

``mode="stub"`` (default): write a deterministic ``# <title>\n\n<description>`` draft
without calling the LLM (llm_wiki parity — review-create-page.ts Create Page button).
``mode="generate"``: the existing full-LLM generation path (explicit secondary action).

embeddings_enabled.title: Embeddings Enabled
embeddings_enabled.description: Whether the embedding data plane is active (EMBEDDINGS_ENABLED env, default true). When false, retrieval degrades to lexical/keyword-only and no embedding service is required at startup (ADR-0030, Feature B). Never exposes the embedding API key.

size.title: Size
size.description: Number of member pages in this community

cohesion.title: Cohesion
cohesion.description: Intra-edge density [0,1]: intraEdges / (size*(size-1)/2). 0 for singletons (no possible edges). Null until first recompute.

cohesion_warning.title: Cohesion Warning
cohesion_warning.description: True when cohesion < GRAPH_COHESION_WARN (default 0.2). Signals a loosely-connected / potentially fragmented community.

members.title: Members
members.description: Member pages ordered by degree descending, capped at 100. Each entry carries id, title, page_type, degree.

GraphCommunityDetailResponse.title: GraphCommunityDetailResponse
GraphCommunityDetailResponse.description:
Response for GET /graph/communities/{community_id} (R9-5, AC-R9-5-1).

Reads from the cached GraphSnapshot — never triggers a recompute (I2).
Returns 409 when the snapshot is cold (cache has never run).

cohesion formula:  cohesion = intraEdges / (size*(size-1)/2)
  intraEdges = number of edges in the snapshot whose both endpoints belong to
               this community.  possibleEdges = size*(size-1)/2.
  cohesion in [0,1].  Singletons (size=1) -> cohesion=0, no possible edges.
  cohesion_warning = True when cohesion < GRAPH_COHESION_WARN (default 0.2, env
  GRAPH_COHESION_WARN).  See engine.py CommunitySnapshot for the per-recompute
  computation; this endpoint reads the value stored in the snapshot (no re-derive).

title.title: Title
title.description: Page title (may be None if not yet set)

degree.title: Degree
degree.description: Structural degree within the full graph

GraphCommunityMemberResponse.title: GraphCommunityMemberResponse
GraphCommunityMemberResponse.description: One member page in a community drill-down response (R9-5).

label.title: Label
label.description: Human-readable community name for Community Mode (F18). Priority: dominant_domain → top_page.title → 'Comunità {id}'. Computed server-side alongside cohesion (I2).

dominant_domain.title: Dominant Domain
dominant_domain.description: Most-frequent in-vocabulary domain/* tag among community members (F18). Prefix 'domain/' is stripped. None when no valid domain tags are present.

top_page.description: Highest-degree member page {id, title, slug} — used for label fallback and tooltip (F18). None when the community has no members.

GraphCommunityResponse.title: GraphCommunityResponse
GraphCommunityResponse.description:
Per-community summary in the GET /graph response (G-P0-2, F18).

id             : re-numbered Louvain community id (0 = largest community).
size           : number of member nodes.
cohesion       : intra-edge density in [0, 1]; 0 for singleton communities.
                 Low cohesion (<0.1) signals a loosely-connected community
                 suitable for a warning in the client legend.
label          : human-readable display name for Community Mode (F18).
                 Priority: dominant_domain → top_page.title → "Comunità {id}".
                 Computed server-side in recompute() — no client computation (I2).
dominant_domain: most-frequent in-vocab "domain/<Name>" tag among members (F18).
                 None when no valid domain tags are present.
top_page       : highest-degree member page {id, title, slug} for disambiguation
                 and tooltip (F18). None for communities with no members.

GraphCommunityTopPageResponse.title: GraphCommunityTopPageResponse
GraphCommunityTopPageResponse.description: Highest-degree member page of a community — disambiguation + tooltip (F18).

kind.title: Kind
kind.description: Structural edge kind: "link" (direct wikilink) | "source" (shared provenance). ADR-0016 §4

GraphEdgeResponse.title: GraphEdgeResponse
GraphEdgeResponse.description:
One graph edge in the GET /graph response (ADR-0014 §6, AC-F4-3, ADR-0016 §4).

source/target are page-id strings (UUID). Undirected — emitted once per pair.
kind: structural edge discriminator — "link" (wikilink exists) or "source"
      (shared-source provenance only). ADR-0016 §4.

breakdown.title: Breakdown
breakdown.description: Per-signal weight components: {direct_links, shared_sources, adamic_adar, type_affinity}. direct_links = 3*direct_count; shared_sources = 4*shared_count; adamic_adar = 1.5*AA(A,B); type_affinity = matrix(typeA,typeB).

computed_at.title: Computed At
computed_at.description: ISO datetime when this edge row was last persisted by the graph engine.

GraphEdgeSignalsResponse.title: GraphEdgeSignalsResponse
GraphEdgeSignalsResponse.description:
4-signal weight breakdown for a single graph edge (R9-5, AC-R9-5-4).

Reads from the persisted edges.signals JSONB column (populated by
GraphEngine.recompute() at every FA2 run).  Never triggers a recompute (I2).

signal semantics (ADR-0012):
  direct_links   = 3.0 * direct_link_count   (wikilink coefficient x3)
  shared_sources = 4.0 * shared_source_count  (provenance coefficient x4)
  adamic_adar    = 1.5 * AA(A,B)             (AA coefficient x1.5)
  type_affinity  = type_affinity_matrix(A,B)  (type-affinity, x1 -- see engine.py)
  total weight   = sum of all four signals

degree.title: Degree
degree.description: Structural degree: count of distinct incident structural edges (direct-link or shared-source); drives size (ADR-0016 §2/§4)

community.title: Community
community.description: Louvain community id (G-P0-2, I2). Re-numbered by size (0 = largest). -1 when not yet assigned (first recompute pending).

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: The node page's own dominant in-vocabulary domain (F18, ADR-0054 §2.2). Prefix 'domain/' is stripped. None when the page has no in-vocab domain tags. Tie-break: when ≥2 in-vocab domain tags are present, the one with the smallest index in the active vocabulary list wins (deterministic). Computed inside the graph recompute — no extra query (I1), cached with the snapshot (I2). Additive field — backward-compatible (default None).

GraphNodeResponse.title: GraphNodeResponse
GraphNodeResponse.description:
One graph node in the GET /graph response (ADR-0014 §6, AC-F4-3, ADR-0016 §4).

Required: id, title, type, x, y.
Optional rendering hints (derived server-side): size, degree.
community: Louvain community id (G-P0-2); -1 when not yet assigned.
domain: the node page's own dominant in-vocabulary domain (F18, ADR-0054 §2.2).
  Stripped of the "domain/" prefix.  None when the page has no in-vocab domain tags.
  Tie-break: when ≥2 in-vocab domain tags exist, first in vocabulary order wins.
  Backward-compatible additive field (default None).

communities.title: Communities
communities.description: Per-community summary (G-P0-2, I2): id, size, cohesion. Ordered by id (0 = largest). Empty until first graph recompute.

total_nodes.title: Total Nodes
total_nodes.description: Count of ALL live (non-deleted) pages in the vault (the full denominator). Use len(nodes) for the in-graph numerator: e.g. '816/986 pages' (GR1). Computed via a bounded indexed COUNT query — I1.

total_edges.title: Total Edges
total_edges.description: Count of ALL link rows for this vault (the full wikilink denominator). Use len(edges) for the in-graph numerator: e.g. '1024/4213 edges' (GR1). Computed via a bounded indexed COUNT query — I1.

GraphResponse.title: GraphResponse
GraphResponse.description:
GET /graph response payload (ADR-0014 §6, AC-F4-3, AC-D4v3-1).

cached     : true on a HIT (no FA2 this request), false on a MISS (FA2 ran inline).
communities: Louvain community summary list (G-P0-2); empty until first recompute.
Header X-Graph-Cache: hit|miss mirrors cached (ADR-0014 §5).

allowed_extensions.title: Allowed Extensions
allowed_extensions.description: Comma-separated extensions to import; '' → default wider set. P3-c.

excluded_folders.title: Excluded Folders
excluded_folders.description: Comma-separated folder names to skip; '' → none excluded. P3-c.

max_size_mb.title: Max Size Mb
max_size_mb.description: Max file size in MB; 0 → no cap. P3-c.

allowed_extensions.title: Allowed Extensions
allowed_extensions.description: Comma-separated extensions the scan imports (e.g. '.pdf,.csv'). null → default wider set (text + all extractable). P3-c.

excluded_folders.title: Excluded Folders
excluded_folders.description: Comma-separated folder names skipped during the scan. null → none. P3-c.

max_size_mb.title: Max Size Mb
max_size_mb.description: Max file size in MB the scan imports; larger skipped. null → no cap. P3-c.

last_run_at.title: Last Run At
last_run_at.description: Timestamp of the last completed scan; null if never run

last_status.title: Last Status
last_status.description: ok | error | running | skipped_disabled | dir_missing | null

last_imported_count.title: Last Imported Count
last_imported_count.description: Files copied (new/changed) during the last scan

last_error.title: Last Error
last_error.description: Error detail from the last failed scan; null on success

dir_ok.title: Dir Ok
dir_ok.description: False if source_dir does not exist/is not readable inside the container

dir_message.title: Dir Message
dir_message.description: Warning message when dir_ok is False; null when ok

ImportSchedulePutResponse.title: ImportSchedulePutResponse
ImportSchedulePutResponse.description:
PUT /import-schedule response body (ADR-0020 §4.6).

Extends ImportScheduleResponse with dir validation fields (save-then-warn).

allowed_extensions.title: Allowed Extensions
allowed_extensions.description: Comma-separated extensions the scan imports (e.g. '.pdf,.csv'). null → default wider set (text + all extractable). P3-c.

excluded_folders.title: Excluded Folders
excluded_folders.description: Comma-separated folder names skipped during the scan. null → none. P3-c.

max_size_mb.title: Max Size Mb
max_size_mb.description: Max file size in MB the scan imports; larger skipped. null → no cap. P3-c.

last_run_at.title: Last Run At
last_run_at.description: Timestamp of the last completed scan; null if never run

last_status.title: Last Status
last_status.description: ok | error | running | skipped_disabled | dir_missing | null

last_imported_count.title: Last Imported Count
last_imported_count.description: Files copied (new/changed) during the last scan

last_error.title: Last Error
last_error.description: Error detail from the last failed scan; null on success

ImportScheduleResponse.title: ImportScheduleResponse
ImportScheduleResponse.description:
GET /import-schedule response body (ADR-0020 §4.6).

Returns the current config + last-run status for the vault's import schedule.
Returns sane defaults (enabled=false, frequency="1h") if no row exists yet.

text.title: Text
text.description: Raw text to ingest (e.g. an assistant message)

source_hint.title: Source Hint
source_hint.description: Optional hint for the output filename stem, e.g. a message_id or short slug. Sanitised to basename; falls back to 'chat-<uuid>' when omitted or unsafe.

IngestFromTextRequest.title: IngestFromTextRequest
IngestFromTextRequest.description:
Request body for POST /ingest/from-text (ADR-0019 §2.7, AC-F6-5 save-to-wiki).

Writes ``text`` to ``vault/raw/sources/chat-{message_id}.md`` (or a derived name)
and runs the same ``ingest_file`` seam (ADR-0003).  No new ingest logic — only a
file-materialisation step.

example.text:
# Homelab notes
Docker services on TrueNAS...

page_id.title: Page Id
page_id.description: Page UUID when ingest completes synchronously (trigger path); null when async (watcher path).

IngestRunListResponse.title: IngestRunListResponse
IngestRunListResponse.description:
Paginated list response for GET /ingest/runs (ADR-0018 §7, AC-BE-IR-1).
Ordered started_at DESC (AC-BE-IR-3).

pages_created.title: Pages Created
pages_created.description: Wiki pages persisted during this run

page_type_counts.title: Page Type Counts
page_type_counts.description: Per-PageType created-page counts; null for legacy/unavailable runs

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.description: Non-convergence diagnostics (1.9.1 W5, NC-1): {stop_reason: 'converged'|'max_iter'|'token_budget', iterations, last_errors, tokens_used, token_budget}. Populated on the orchestrated/block routes for every terminal outcome; null for the delegated/CLI route (no bounded loop) and legacy rows.

total_cost_usd.title: Total Cost Usd
total_cost_usd.description: Total cost in USD; 0.0 for local/cli; serialised as number (I7)

completed_at.title: Completed At
completed_at.description: Run finish time (aliases finished_at); null for running rows

error_message.title: Error Message
error_message.description: Error detail for failed runs; null otherwise

IngestRunResponse.title: IngestRunResponse
IngestRunResponse.description:
API response shape for one ingest_runs row (ADR-0018 §7, AC-BE-IR-1).

Column aliases (no DB rename — ADR-0018 §7 decision):
  max_iter_used  → iterations_used
  finished_at    → completed_at
total_cost_usd serialised as a float; frontend formats to exactly 4dp (I7).

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Async task UUID (None in v0.2 synchronous mode; filled in v0.3+)

page_id.title: Page Id
page_id.description: UUID of the ingested page row

IngestTriggerResponse.title: IngestTriggerResponse
IngestTriggerResponse.description:
Typed 202 body for POST /ingest/trigger (AC-D4u — task_id appears in OpenAPI schema).

task_id is None in v0.2 (synchronous path); v0.3 fills it with a real async task UUID.
status: "completed" | "skipped" (I1 fast-path) | "queued"/"running" (async, v0.3+).

severity_totals.title: Severity Totals
severity_totals.description: COUNT(*) per severity (info/warning/error) over the same vault + status + category predicate as 'total', but IGNORING the severity filter and pagination. Use this to render accurate group-header counts (e.g. 'Warnings (N)') even when a severity filter is active. Keys present only for severities with at least one matching finding.

category.title: Category
category.description: orphan-page | broken-wikilink | missing-xref | contradiction | stale-claim | missing-page

proposed_action.title: Proposed Action
proposed_action.description: Fix apply_lint_fix would attempt; null for flag-only findings

suggested_target.title: Suggested Target
suggested_target.description: Title of the best-matching existing page (broken-wikilink only). Displayed as the green 'Suggested target:' strip in the lint UI (L2).

suggested_page_id.title: Suggested Page Id
suggested_page_id.description: FK → pages.id for the suggested_target page (L2).

LintFindingResponse.title: LintFindingResponse
LintFindingResponse.description: API response shape for one lint_findings row (ADR-0037 §6).

LintRunResponse.title: LintRunResponse
LintRunResponse.description: API response shape for one lint_runs row (ADR-0037 §6).

max_iter.title: Max Iter
max_iter.description: Max semantic rounds (1..10); null → LINT_MAX_ITER default

semantic.title: Semantic
semantic.description: When False, skip the provider pass entirely (deterministic findings only; zero LLM cost). L8 / ADR-0037 B1.

LintScanRequest.title: LintScanRequest
LintScanRequest.description:
Request body for POST /lint/scan (ADR-0037 §6).

max_iter and token_budget are optional — env defaults (LINT_MAX_ITER / LINT_TOKEN_BUDGET)
apply when omitted. Both are FROZEN onto the lint_runs row before the scan runs (I7).
Server-side validators cap the range so callers cannot request an unbounded run (I7).
semantic: when False, skip the provider pass entirely (deterministic findings only;
run records iterations_used=0, cost=0). L8 / ADR-0037 B1.

LintScanResponse.title: LintScanResponse
LintScanResponse.description: 200 response for POST /lint/scan (ADR-0037 §6): the run + its findings.

detail.title: Detail
detail.description: Error detail when status='failed'; null otherwise

companion_path.title: Companion Path
companion_path.description: Vault-relative path to the written .extracted.md (raw/sources/<stem>.extracted.md). Set when status='ok'; null otherwise.

batch_id.title: Batch Id
batch_id.description: UUID of the current/most-recent batch; null if no batch has run

running.title: Running
running.description: True while the background driver is active

eta_seconds.title: Eta Seconds
eta_seconds.description: Estimated seconds remaining (null when done=0 or batch finished)

files.title: Files
files.description: Per-file status entries (empty when no batch has run)

MarkerBatchStatusResponse.title: MarkerBatchStatusResponse
MarkerBatchStatusResponse.description:
Response for GET /ingest/convert-marker/status.

Returns the current (or most-recent) batch state with per-file detail.
When no batch has ever run this session, running=false and all counts are 0.

queued.title: Queued
queued.description: Files accepted and queued for background Marker conversion

MarkerConvertAcceptResponse.title: MarkerConvertAcceptResponse
MarkerConvertAcceptResponse.description:
202 Accepted response for POST /ingest/convert-marker (W0 async rewrite).

The Marker calls happen in a background task — poll
GET /ingest/convert-marker/status (or GET /ingest/queue .marker_batch) for progress.

safe_stem.title: Safe Stem
safe_stem.description: Sanitised stem used for the output paths (e.g. 'report')

pdf_path.title: Pdf Path
pdf_path.description: Vault-relative path where the raw PDF was saved (raw/sources/<stem>.pdf)

MarkerConvertAcceptedFile.title: MarkerConvertAcceptedFile
MarkerConvertAcceptedFile.description: One file entry in the 202 response body of POST /ingest/convert-marker.

status.title: Status
status.description: "ok" when Marker responds 200; "offline" when unreachable or non-200.

detail.title: Detail
detail.description: Error detail when status='offline'; null when ok.

rotate_token.title: Rotate Token
rotate_token.description: true ⇒ generate a new high-entropy token (secrets.token_urlsafe(32)), store its PBKDF2 hash, return plaintext ONCE in generated_token. The plaintext is NEVER stored and NEVER returned again.

token.title: Token
token.description: Owner-supplied explicit token; stored as PBKDF2 hash only. generated_token stays null (owner already knows the value). Not echoed in the response.

clear_token.title: Clear Token
clear_token.description: true ⇒ set mcp_access_token_hash = NULL. If this leaves token_configured=false AND allow_without_token=false, remote_enabled is clamped OFF (no usable auth posture).

allow_without_token.title: Allow Without Token
allow_without_token.description: Persist the allow-without-token flag (ADR-0033 §2.3). Omit to leave unchanged. true: private sources (loopback/CGNAT/RFC1918/link-local) may connect without a bearer token. PUBLIC sources are NEVER exempted.

McpAuthRequest.title: McpAuthRequest
McpAuthRequest.description:
Request body for PUT /mcp/auth (ADR-0033 §2.5).

All fields are optional; omitting a field leaves that aspect unchanged.
Exactly one of rotate_token / token / clear_token should be used per call
(using multiple is allowed but the last write wins: clear > explicit > rotate).
allow_without_token can be set independently in the same call.

token_configured.title: Token Configured
token_configured.description: True iff DB hash is set OR MCP_AUTH_TOKEN env bootstrap is set.

allow_without_token.title: Allow Without Token
allow_without_token.description: The persisted allow-without-token flag after this write.

remote_enabled.title: Remote Enabled
remote_enabled.description: The remote_mcp_enabled flag after any allow-aware clamp.

generated_token.title: Generated Token
generated_token.description: Populated ONLY when rotate_token=true — the plaintext token shown ONCE. null in all other cases. NEVER stored; NEVER returned by subsequent calls.

McpAuthStateResponse.title: McpAuthStateResponse
McpAuthStateResponse.description:
Response body for PUT /mcp/auth (ADR-0033 §2.5).

token_configured, token_source, allow_without_token, remote_enabled, mount_path
always reflect the post-write state. generated_token is populated ONLY when
rotate_token=true was set — shown ONCE, never returned again by any GET/PUT.
NEVER contains the token, hash, or salt.

server_name.title: Server Name
server_name.description: MCP server name, derived from the live FastMCP object (I6)

transport.title: Transport
transport.description: MCP transport type (MCP_TRANSPORT env, default 'stdio'; ADR-0010)

entry_point_command.title: Entry Point Command
entry_point_command.description: Command to launch the MCP server (MCP_ENTRY_COMMAND env; ADR-0027 §2.3)

tool_count.title: Tool Count
tool_count.description: Number of tools currently registered in the server

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Introspected tool list from the live registry

http_enabled.title: Http Enabled
http_enabled.description: Whether the HTTP MCP surface is compiled in and mounted (ADR-0029 §2.2). Always true (ADR-0033 §2.4 always-mount). The token itself is never returned. Alias: token_configured for backward compat.

remote_write_enabled.title: Remote Write Enabled
remote_write_enabled.description: Whether write tools (write_page, resolve_review, trigger_source_rescan) are enabled on the HTTP surface (ADR-0029 §2.3, ADR-0072). Reflects the effective runtime flag: vault_state.remote_mcp_write_enabled when non-NULL (DB-wins), else MCP_REMOTE_WRITE_ENABLED env (default false). Toggle via PUT /mcp/remote-write. Only meaningful when remote_enabled is true.

token_configured.title: Token Configured
token_configured.description: True iff a token is configured — DB hash set OR MCP_AUTH_TOKEN env set (ADR-0033 §2.1 precedence). NEVER contains the token value.

remote_enabled.title: Remote Enabled
remote_enabled.description: The persisted runtime toggle state from vault_state.remote_mcp_enabled (ADR-0032 §2.1). False by default; can be set via PUT /mcp/remote.

mount_path.title: Mount Path
mount_path.description: The mount path for the remote MCP HTTP surface (= MCP_MOUNT_PATH constant). UI builds the connection URL as: window.location.origin + mount_path (ADR-0032 §2.5; I6 — derived from constant, never hardcoded in handler).

token_source.title: Token Source
token_source.description: "db" | "env" | "none" — which token source is authoritative (ADR-0033 §2.1). "db": UI-set token (PBKDF2 hash in vault_state). "env": MCP_AUTH_TOKEN env bootstrap. "none": no token configured. NEVER the token value, hash, or salt.

allow_without_token.title: Allow Without Token
allow_without_token.description: Whether token-less access is permitted for PRIVATE sources (loopback/CGNAT/RFC1918/link-local/ULA — ADR-0033 §2.3). PUBLIC sources (Cloudflare tunnel) are NEVER exempted regardless of this flag.

McpInfoResponse.title: McpInfoResponse
McpInfoResponse.description: Response model for GET /mcp/info (ADR-0027 §2.1; ADR-0029 §2.5; ADR-0032 §2.5; ADR-0033).

remote_enabled.title: Remote Enabled
remote_enabled.description: The resulting persisted runtime flag (post-clamp). False when clamped=true.

token_configured.title: Token Configured
token_configured.description: True iff MCP_AUTH_TOKEN is set (the security floor). NEVER contains the token value.

mount_path.title: Mount Path
mount_path.description: Mount path for the remote MCP HTTP surface (= MCP_MOUNT_PATH constant; I6).

clamped.title: Clamped
clamped.description: True iff the request asked enabled=true but MCP_AUTH_TOKEN is unset — the flag was forced to false (token-floor clamp, ADR-0032 §2.4).

McpRemoteStateResponse.title: McpRemoteStateResponse
McpRemoteStateResponse.description:
Response model for PUT /mcp/remote (ADR-0032 §2.4).

Always returned with HTTP 200 (even when clamped — the posture is reported truthfully).
The token itself is NEVER returned (I6).

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Desired runtime state for the remote MCP HTTP surface.

remote_write_enabled.title: Remote Write Enabled
remote_write_enabled.description: The resulting persisted runtime flag (post-clamp). False when clamped=true.

token_configured.title: Token Configured
token_configured.description: True iff a token is configured (DB hash or env bootstrap — ADR-0033 §2.1). NEVER contains the token value.

clamped.title: Clamped
clamped.description: True iff the request asked enabled=true but neither a token is configured nor allow_without_token is set — the flag was forced to false (token-floor clamp, ADR-0072 §4).

McpRemoteWriteStateResponse.title: McpRemoteWriteStateResponse
McpRemoteWriteStateResponse.description:
Response model for PUT /mcp/remote-write (ADR-0072 §4).

Always returned with HTTP 200 (even when clamped — the posture is reported truthfully).

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Desired runtime state for the write tools on the HTTP MCP surface.

name.title: Name
name.description: Tool name as registered in the FastMCP server

input_schema.title: Input Schema
input_schema.description: JSON-Schema object for the tool's input arguments (tool.parameters)

McpToolInfo.title: McpToolInfo
McpToolInfo.description: Schema for a single tool entry in GET /mcp/info (ADR-0027 §2.1).

OpenProjectRequest.title: OpenProjectRequest
OpenProjectRequest.description: Body for POST /projects/open — register an existing vault folder.

last_run_at.title: Last Run At
last_run_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last completed run, or null.

last_detail.title: Last Detail
last_detail.description: Short human outcome of the last run ('12 tagged / 30 processed', 'dormant: no domain vocabulary configured', 'error: ...'), or null. R13-12.

in_flight.title: In Flight
in_flight.description: True while this op is currently executing.

OpsScheduleEntry.title: OpsScheduleEntry
OpsScheduleEntry.description: One row in the GET /ops/schedules response — state of one schedulable op.

content.title: Content
content.description: Full UTF-8 markdown content to write

expected_hash.title: Expected Hash
expected_hash.description: Optimistic concurrency guard — sha256 hex of the content the client last read. When provided and it does NOT match the current on-disk hash, 409 is returned so the editor can warn about a stale edit.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Frontmatter 'tags[]' navigation tags; NULL if absent (K6)

PageContentResponse.title: PageContentResponse
PageContentResponse.description:
Response for GET /pages/{id}/content (F1-content-read).

Additive extension (backward-compatible): page_type (serialised as "type"), sources, and
tags are included so the reader can render a type badge, sources list, and navigation
tags without a second call to GET /pages/{id}.  All three are nullable (NULL when absent
from YAML frontmatter).  tags is the K6 navigation list (nashsu/llm_wiki parity), mirroring
the sources column.

dir.title: Dir
dir.description: Target subdirectory under wiki/ (optional; derived from page_type when omitted). Allowed values: ['comparisons', 'concepts', 'entities', 'queries', 'sources', 'synthesis']

PageCreateRequest.title: PageCreateRequest
PageCreateRequest.description:
Request body for POST /pages (R7-2, AC-R7-2-2, [F1]).

Creates a new wiki page with minimal frontmatter via the shared write_wiki_page seam (I1).
A 409 is returned when a live page with the same (vault_id, file_path) already exists.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: Dominant vocabulary domain derived server-side from 'domain/<name>' tags. null when the page is untagged or no domain vocabulary is configured. Reuses the same derivation logic as GET /stats/sections (stats.py).

community.title: Community
community.description: Louvain community id persisted by GraphEngine.recompute(). null until the first graph recompute after migration 0020 (G-P0-2, I2).

resolved.title: Resolved
resolved.description: True → resolve (action required); False → reopen to pending. Default True.

action.title: Action
action.description: Resolution action when resolved=True: skip | dismiss (exact tokens from ops/review.py; 422 on unknown). Ignored when resolved=False (reopen).

PatchReviewRequest.title: PatchReviewRequest
PatchReviewRequest.description: Request body for PATCH /review/queue/{id} (B5/D2 — llm_wiki parity).

Project.title: Project
Project.description: One known project (vault). ``id`` is the vault_id used for its DB/Qdrant rows.

ProjectsResponse.title: ProjectsResponse
ProjectsResponse.description: Response for GET /projects — the registry snapshot.

operation.title: Operation
operation.description: ingest | chat | lint; required when scope='operation'

model_id.title: Model Id
model_id.description: Model name (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6); lives only in DB rows (AC-F17-8)

base_url.title: Base Url
base_url.description: OpenAI-compatible endpoint; NULL for Anthropic/local default

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: WRITE-ONLY (W1). Plaintext provider API key; encrypted at rest (Fernet, SYNAPSE_SECRET_KEY). NEVER returned by any response. Omit to use env-var keys. Requires SYNAPSE_SECRET_KEY configured server-side (else HTTP 400).

ProviderConfigCreate.title: ProviderConfigCreate
ProviderConfigCreate.description:
Request body for POST /provider/config (F17, W1).

api_key is WRITE-ONLY (W1, §12 amendment): the plaintext is encrypted at rest
(SYNAPSE_SECRET_KEY / Fernet) and NEVER returned by any response. Omit it to keep env-var
keys. model_id must be provided explicitly — no hardcoded defaults in app code (AC-F17-8).

api_key_configured.title: Api Key Configured
api_key_configured.description: True iff a UI API key is stored (encrypted) for this row. Never the value.

api_key_masked.title: Api Key Masked
api_key_masked.description: Non-reversible masked hint ('…1234') when a key is stored; null otherwise.

ProviderConfigResponse.title: ProviderConfigResponse
ProviderConfigResponse.description:
API response shape for a provider_config row (§12 amendment, W1).

NEVER exposes the plaintext key. Instead: api_key_configured (bool) + api_key_masked
("…last4" hint, or null). reasoning_effort is echoed (config, not a secret).

model_id.title: Model Id
model_id.description: Model name (lives only in DB rows)

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: WRITE-ONLY (W1). Non-empty ⇒ replace stored key (encrypted). Empty string ⇒ clear the stored key (env fallback). Omit to leave unchanged. NEVER returned.

ProviderConfigUpdate.title: ProviderConfigUpdate
ProviderConfigUpdate.description:
Request body for PUT /provider/config/{id} (W1). All fields optional — omitted fields are
left unchanged.

api_key semantics (WRITE-ONLY): field ABSENT ⇒ leave the stored key untouched; a non-empty
string ⇒ re-encrypt and replace; an empty string "" ⇒ CLEAR the stored key (fall back to
env). The plaintext is NEVER returned.

config_id.title: Config Id
config_id.description: Probe an existing provider_config row (uses its stored key).

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: WRITE-ONLY inline key; overrides the stored/env key. Never echoed.

ProviderTestRequest.title: ProviderTestRequest
ProviderTestRequest.description:
Request body for the provider-test endpoints (W1).

Provide EITHER config_id (probe a stored row — its decrypted key is used) OR inline
{provider_type, model, base_url?, api_key?}. Inline fields override the stored row when both
are given. api_key is WRITE-ONLY — never echoed back.

ok.title: Ok
ok.description: True iff the probe succeeded (connection: HTTP ok; function: reply matched).

latency_ms.title: Latency Ms
latency_ms.description: Wall-clock latency of the probe in milliseconds.

detail.title: Detail
detail.description: Human-readable outcome. NEVER contains the API key.

ProviderTestResponse.title: ProviderTestResponse
ProviderTestResponse.description: Response body for the provider-test endpoints (W1). Never contains a key.

running.title: Running
running.description: True while an ingest-all batch is in progress

done.title: Done
done.description: Files processed so far in the current batch

total.title: Total
total.description: Total files in the current batch

eta_seconds.title: Eta Seconds
eta_seconds.description: Estimated seconds remaining for the whole batch (None if unknown)

QueueBatchProgress.title: QueueBatchProgress
QueueBatchProgress.description:
Whole-batch progress for a POST /sources/ingest-all run (surfaced in the activity panel).

Lets the UI show "done/total" + a total ETA for the entire bulk index, not just the
one-file-at-a-time queue view. eta_seconds = remaining files × avg per-file duration
(best-effort; None when no history).

cleaned_pages.title: Cleaned Pages
cleaned_pages.description: Always 0 at request time; cascade cleanup happens at the next loop boundary

drained.title: Drained
drained.description: Number of pending entries replayed to the watcher

run_id_prev.title: Run Id Prev
run_id_prev.description: UUID of the failed run that was retried

retry_count.title: Retry Count
retry_count.description: New retry count (1..3); I7 hard cap = 3

paused.title: Paused
paused.description: True when the queue is paused (no new dispatches)

pending.title: Pending
pending.description: Number of FS events parked while queue is paused

processing.title: Processing
processing.description: Number of currently in-flight ingest runs

failed.title: Failed
failed.description: Number of recently-failed tasks retained for retry

completed_since_idle.title: Completed Since Idle
completed_since_idle.description: Successful completions since the queue last went idle

batch.description: Whole-batch progress for an in-progress POST /sources/ingest-all (else null)

marker_batch.description: Whole-batch progress for an in-progress POST /ingest/convert-marker (else null). Poll GET /ingest/convert-marker/status for per-file detail.

QueueSnapshotResponse.title: QueueSnapshotResponse
QueueSnapshotResponse.description: Live activity queue snapshot for GET /ingest/queue (ADR-0046 §6).

run_id.title: Run Id
run_id.description: UUID of the in-flight run; null for pending (not yet dispatched) tasks

retry_count.title: Retry Count
retry_count.description: Times this source has been retried (I7, max 3)

error.title: Error
error.description: Error detail for failed tasks; null otherwise

started_at.title: Started At
started_at.description: ISO-8601 start time; null for pending tasks

phase.title: Phase
phase.description: Human-facing current phase: queued | analyzing | generating (N/M) | validating | writing | agent running. None for pending/failed tasks.

progress.title: Progress
progress.description: Coarse 0..1 progress for orchestrated runs (0.0=queued, 0.2=analyzing, 0.5=generating, 0.8=validating, 0.95=writing). None for delegated/CLI runs (indeterminate spinner) and non-processing tasks.

elapsed_seconds.title: Elapsed Seconds
elapsed_seconds.description: Seconds since the run started; null for pending/failed tasks.

eta_seconds.title: Eta Seconds
eta_seconds.description: Best-effort estimated seconds until completion, derived from the historical average duration for this route (last 50 completed runs). None when no history is available for the route or for non-processing tasks.

QueueTaskItem.title: QueueTaskItem
QueueTaskItem.description: One task in the live ingest queue snapshot (ADR-0046 §6).

max_pages.title: Max Pages
max_pages.description: Cap on pages processed this run (clamped server-side).

token_budget.title: Token Budget
token_budget.description: Token budget for the run (clamped server-side, I7).

force.title: Force
force.description: Widen candidates from the suspicious set (NULL/untyped/concept) to ALL non-reserved wiki pages (overview/index are never touched in either mode).

max_pages.title: Max Pages
max_pages.description: Effective (clamped) page cap for this run

token_budget.title: Token Budget
token_budget.description: Effective (clamped) token budget for this run

force.title: Force
force.description: Whether ALL non-reserved pages are candidates

running.title: Running
running.description: True while a reclassify run is in flight

last_summary.title: Last Summary
last_summary.description: Summary of the most recent completed run (null if never ran)

ReclassifyTypesStatusResponse.title: ReclassifyTypesStatusResponse
ReclassifyTypesStatusResponse.description: GET /ops/reclassify-types — single-flight state + last completed summary.

max_pages.title: Max Pages
max_pages.description: Cap on wiki pages scanned per run (clamped server-side, I7).

apply.title: Apply
apply.description: False (default) = dry-run only — returns a plan with no file writes. True = perform actual moves + DB + Qdrant updates.

max_pages.title: Max Pages
max_pages.description: Effective (clamped) page cap for this run

running.title: Running
running.description: True while a reconcile run is in flight

last_summary.title: Last Summary
last_summary.description: Summary of the most recent completed run (null if never ran). Includes 'plan' (list of proposed moves) in dry-run mode.

ReconcileFoldersStatusResponse.title: ReconcileFoldersStatusResponse
ReconcileFoldersStatusResponse.description: GET /ops/reconcile-folders — single-flight state + last completed summary.

ReferencedPage.title: ReferencedPage
ReferencedPage.description: Convenience join for a referenced_page_ids entry (ADR-0044 §6.1).

reconnected.title: Reconnected
reconnected.description: Previously-dangling wikilinks reconnected to a live page this pass.

remaining_dangling.title: Remaining Dangling
remaining_dangling.description: Wikilinks still dangling after the reconnect pass.

nodes.title: Nodes
nodes.description: Node count in the freshly recomputed snapshot.

edges.title: Edges
edges.description: Edge count in the freshly recomputed snapshot.

regenerated.title: Regenerated
regenerated.description: Whether overview.md was rewritten this call.

detected_language.title: Detected Language
detected_language.description: Language the overview was regenerated in (ISO-639-1) or null.

RegenerateOverviewResponse.title: RegenerateOverviewResponse
RegenerateOverviewResponse.description:
Response for POST /overview/regenerate (F3 — force an overview refresh without re-ingesting).

regenerated: True if the overview.md note was rewritten this call (False = degrade-safe
             keep-previous, e.g. no provider configured or the provider call failed/timed out).
detected_language: the language the overview was requested in (ISO-639-1) — from
                   the vault's existing pages; None when undetectable.

title.title: Title
title.description: YAML frontmatter title; NULL if absent

type.title: Type
type.description: YAML frontmatter type; NULL if absent

RelatedPageItem.title: RelatedPageItem
RelatedPageItem.description:
One entry in the GET /pages/{id}/related response.

score is the stored 4-signal edge weight (ADR-0012):
  3·direct_link_count + 4·shared_source_count + 1.5·adamic_adar + 1·same_type
Reuses the persisted edges table — no recompute (I1/I2).

reconnected.title: Reconnected
reconnected.description: Previously-dangling links reconnected to a live page in this pass.

remaining_dangling.title: Remaining Dangling
remaining_dangling.description: Links still dangling after the pass (target has no matching live page).

ReresolveLinksResponse.title: ReresolveLinksResponse
ReresolveLinksResponse.description:
Response for POST /links/reresolve (F3/K3 cross-ingest connectivity backfill).

reconnected: number of previously-dangling links now bound to a live page.
remaining_dangling: number of links still dangling after the pass.

vault_id.title: Vault Id
vault_id.description: Vault scope used to resolve the provider AND to load overview.md/purpose.md context. null → the server's default vault_id (settings.vault_id).

ResearchOptimizeTopicRequest.title: ResearchOptimizeTopicRequest
ResearchOptimizeTopicRequest.description:
Request body for POST /research/optimize-topic (B5/D3).

A seed topic (typically taken from a Graph Insight) to be rephrased into a domain-specific
research topic + web-search-optimized queries before the user confirms a Deep Research run.

optimized_topic.title: Optimized Topic
optimized_topic.description: Domain-specific rephrasing of the seed topic

queries.title: Queries
queries.description: 3..5 web-search-optimized SearXNG queries (or [topic] on the fallback path)

ResearchOptimizeTopicResponse.title: ResearchOptimizeTopicResponse
ResearchOptimizeTopicResponse.description:
200 response for POST /research/optimize-topic (B5/D3).

optimized_topic prefills the editable topic field; queries prefill the editable query list.
On the no-provider / degraded path optimized_topic echoes the seed topic and queries is
[topic] — the dialog still opens (never a 500).

example.optimized_topic: Kubernetes container networking (CNI) — Calico vs Cilium

ResearchRunDetail.title: ResearchRunDetail
ResearchRunDetail.description:
GET /research/runs/{id} response (ADR-0024 §8.3, AC-F10-4c).

Includes the full queries_used array and per-source summaries.
synthesis_text is null until step 5 completes (AC-F10-4c).
sources array excludes fetched_content_md blobs by default (size guard, ADR-0024 §8.3).

ResearchRunSummary.title: ResearchRunSummary
ResearchRunSummary.description:
One item in GET /research/runs (ADR-0024 §8.2, AC-F10-4b).

Mirrors the ingest_runs list shape: id, topic, status, cost, timing.

ResearchSourceSummary.title: ResearchSourceSummary
ResearchSourceSummary.description: One source row in GET /research/runs/{id} (ADR-0024 §8.3, AC-F10-6b).

max_iter.title: Max Iter
max_iter.description: Max refinement rounds (1..10); null → DEEP_RESEARCH_MAX_ITER default

queries.title: Queries
queries.description: Optional caller-supplied SearXNG queries (B5/D3). When present (non-empty) the FIRST iteration uses these VERBATIM instead of the provider query-gen round-trip — the same seed_queries seam a review item uses (bounded to DEEP_RESEARCH_MAX_QUERIES). This is how the 'optimize + confirm' dialog passes the user-edited queries. null/[] → generate queries from scratch (default, unchanged behavior).

ResearchStartRequest.title: ResearchStartRequest
ResearchStartRequest.description:
Request body for POST /research/start (ADR-0024 §8.1, AC-F10-4).

max_iter and token_budget are optional — env defaults apply when omitted.
Both are FROZEN onto the deep_research_runs row before the background task starts
(AQ-v0.5-4, I7). Server-side validators cap the range so callers cannot request an
unbounded run (I7 / Do-NOT #1/#2).

run_id.title: Run Id
run_id.description: UUID of the created deep_research_runs row

ids.title: Ids
ids.description: Review item ids to act on; capped at REVIEW_BULK_MAX_IDS (I7 — 400 over)

updated.title: Updated
updated.description: Pending ids mutated to the new terminal status

skipped_terminal.title: Skipped Terminal
skipped_terminal.description: Ids that were already terminal (or confirm under mark-resolved); never mutated

deleted.title: Deleted
deleted.description: Terminal rows hard-deleted for the vault (pending untouched)

item_type.title: Item Type
item_type.description: missing-page | suggestion | contradiction | duplicate | confirm | purpose-suggestion

status.title: Status
status.description: pending | created | skipped | dismissed | deep_researched | auto_resolved

proposed_title.title: Proposed Title
proposed_title.description: Title the LLM proposes to create; drives lazy skeleton (ADR-0034 §5.2)

proposal_origin.title: Proposal Origin
proposal_origin.description: rule | ai | corpus | system | lint | legacy (ADR-0073)

proposed_dir.title: Proposed Dir
proposed_dir.description: Target wiki/ subdir (display only; recomputed at Create — ADR-0034 §5.2)

rationale.title: Rationale
rationale.description: Why this matters; used as topic hint for Deep Research (ADR-0034 §3.1)

page_id.title: Page Id
page_id.description: Review TARGET: conflicting/context existing page FK (ADR-0034 §3.1)

page_title.title: Page Title
page_title.description: Convenience join from pages.title for page_id (UI display)

source_page_id.title: Source Page Id
source_page_id.description: Provenance: page whose ingest produced this proposal (ADR-0034 §3.1)

created_page_id.title: Created Page Id
created_page_id.description: Page produced by a successful Create action (ADR-0034 §5); null otherwise

created_page_type.title: Created Page Type
created_page_type.description: Effective type joined from created_page_id; null until/no Create

deep_research_run_id.title: Deep Research Run Id
deep_research_run_id.description: FK → deep_research_runs.id; set when Deep-Research fires (AC-F10-5)

content_key.title: Content Key
content_key.description: Stable FNV-1a dedup handle (opaque to UI); NULL for confirm (ADR-0044 §3.2)

referenced_page_ids.title: Referenced Page Ids
referenced_page_ids.description: Array of page-id strings this proposal is contextually about (ADR-0044 §2)

referenced_pages.title: Referenced Pages
referenced_pages.description: Convenience join [{id,title,type}] for referenced_page_ids; stale ids filtered at render (ADR-0044 §6.1/§9.2) so the card renders [[title]] links without a round-trip

search_queries.title: Search Queries
search_queries.description: ≤3 pre-generated search queries; search_queries[0] seeds Deep Research

ReviewItemResponse.title: ReviewItemResponse
ReviewItemResponse.description:
API response shape for one review_items row (ADR-0034 §7.1; ADR-0044 §6.1 additions).

Projection carries the full proposal model: type, proposed_title, proposed_page_type,
proposed_dir, rationale, and the three page FK fields (page_id/source_page_id/created_page_id).
page_title is a convenience join from pages.title for the page_id FK (UI display).
resolution records how the item was closed (null while pending).

ADR-0044 §6.1 adds: content_key (opaque dedup handle), referenced_page_ids (array),
referenced_pages (convenience join, stale ids filtered), search_queries (Deep-Research seeds).

llm_resolved.title: Llm Resolved
llm_resolved.description: Items closed by conservative LLM Pass-2

kept.title: Kept
kept.description: Items that remain pending after the sweep

corpus_proposed.title: Corpus Proposed
corpus_proposed.description: Synthesis/comparison suggestions seeded from the graph in Pass-3 (SC-D3)

sources.title: Sources
sources.description: Optional source references to attach to the page frontmatter

conversation_id.title: Conversation Id
conversation_id.description: Optional conversation UUID for provenance reference

SaveToWikiRequest.title: SaveToWikiRequest
SaveToWikiRequest.description:
Request body for POST /chat/save-to-wiki (G-P0-1; CG-A2/A5, ADR-0067 P3-3).

Saves a cleaned chat assistant answer to the vault. A lightweight classifier routes an open
QUESTION to ``wiki/queries/<slug>.md`` (type=query) and any answer/analysis — the common case
— to ``wiki/synthesis/<slug>.md`` (type=synthesis). The write itself calls no inference
provider (I6); a bounded fire-and-forget wikilink-enrichment pass may run afterwards to
graph-connect the saved page (CG-A3).

example.content: bge-m3 is a multi-lingual dense embedding model...
example.title: What is bge-m3?

page_id.title: Page Id
page_id.description: UUID of the created/updated wiki page

file_path.title: File Path
file_path.description: Relative path in the vault. Usually wiki/synthesis/<slug>.md (an answer/analysis); wiki/queries/<slug>.md when the saved content is itself an open question.

SaveToWikiResponse.title: SaveToWikiResponse
SaveToWikiResponse.description: 201 response for POST /chat/save-to-wiki (G-P0-1; CG-A2/A5, ADR-0067 P3-3).

ScenarioItem.title: ScenarioItem
ScenarioItem.description: One scenario preset descriptor (R7-1 list response).

context.title: Context
context.description: Assembled context string with inline [n] markers (≤ token_budget, ADR-0022)

results.title: Results
results.description: Citations in rank order (vector seeds first, then expansions by edge weight)

data_version.title: Data Version
data_version.description: Snapshot read BEFORE assembly — proves the call is read-only (AC-F5-5)

token_budget.title: Token Budget
token_budget.description: 20% of context_window used as the retrieval slice

SearchResponse.title: SearchResponse
SearchResponse.description:
GET /search response (ADR-0022 §2.5, AC-F5-6).

read-only — never bumps data_version (AC-F5-5).
0-hit → 200 with empty results + empty context (AC-F5-7a).

id.title: Id
id.description: UUID of the pages row (== Qdrant point id, ADR-0002)

title.title: Title
title.description: Frontmatter title or filename stem (never empty, §2.6)

slug.title: Slug
slug.description: slugify(title) — derived, not a DB column (§2.6)

SearchResultItem.title: SearchResultItem
SearchResultItem.description:
One citation entry in the GET /search response (ADR-0022 §2.5, AC-F5-6).

Maps to Citation.{n, ref.id, ref.title, ref.slug, score, phase}.

SourceDeleteResponse.title: SourceDeleteResponse
SourceDeleteResponse.description:
Response for DELETE /sources.

Single-file delete:  deleted_source=<rel_path>, files_deleted=1, pages_cascaded=N,
                     pages_deleted=N (backward-compat alias; always equals pages_cascaded).
Directory delete:    deleted_source=<rel_dir>,  files_deleted=K, pages_cascaded=M,
                     pages_deleted=M.

SourceDerivedPage.title: SourceDerivedPage
SourceDerivedPage.description: One derived page row for GET /sources/derived-pages and GET /sources/content.

SourceEntry.title: SourceEntry
SourceEntry.description: One file or directory entry in the sources tree.

version.title: Version
version.description: Backend package version from pyproject.toml via importlib.metadata (ADR-0054 §6). Read at runtime — never a hardcoded literal.

review_pending.title: Review Pending
review_pending.description: Count of pending review-queue items — feeds the NavRail badge via the existing /status poll (no dedicated poller, I3). Additive, non-breaking.

supports_vision.title: Supports Vision
supports_vision.description: B2-C1: True when the active chat provider reports capabilities().supports_vision. The frontend uses this to gate the attach-image button. Additive, non-breaking — defaults False so existing clients without the field read as vision-disabled.

max_pages.title: Max Pages
max_pages.description: Cap on pages auto-written this run (synthesis+comparison; clamped).

max_candidates.title: Max Candidates
max_candidates.description: Cap on all evaluated clusters, including Review/skip paths (I7).

token_budget.title: Token Budget
token_budget.description: Token budget for the run (clamped server-side, I7).

force.title: Force
force.description: Regenerate/update the same keyed corpus pages; never create duplicates.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: auto writes high-confidence pages; review-only enqueues eligible clusters.

max_pages.title: Max Pages
max_pages.description: Effective (clamped) page cap for this run

token_budget.title: Token Budget
token_budget.description: Effective (clamped) token budget for this run

running.title: Running
running.description: True while a synthesize run is in flight

current.title: Current
current.description: Effective bounds/mode of the active run; null while idle

last_summary.title: Last Summary
last_summary.description: Summary of the most recent completed run (null if never ran)

SynthesizeStatusResponse.title: SynthesizeStatusResponse
SynthesizeStatusResponse.description: GET /ops/synthesize — single-flight state + last completed summary.

triggered.title: Triggered
triggered.description: True when Watchtower accepted the update request.

SystemUpdateResponse.title: SystemUpdateResponse
SystemUpdateResponse.description: Result of poking Watchtower to pull+recreate the labelled containers.

latest_version.title: Latest Version
latest_version.description: Latest published GitHub Release tag (X.Y.Z), or null if unknown.

update_available.title: Update Available
update_available.description: True when latest_version is semver-newer than current_version.

update_supported.title: Update Supported
update_supported.description: True when the Watchtower HTTP API is configured, i.e. POST /ops/system-update can actually trigger an update. When false the UI hides the 'update' button.

UpdateStatusResponse.title: UpdateStatusResponse
UpdateStatusResponse.description: Deployment update availability (read-only; safe to poll).

file_path.title: File Path
file_path.description: Saved path relative to vault_root, e.g. "raw/sources/notes.md"

status.title: Status
status.description: "queued" — file saved to raw/sources/; watcher ingests asynchronously.

overwritten.title: Overwritten
overwritten.description: True if a same-name file already existed and was replaced on disk

UploadResponse.title: UploadResponse
UploadResponse.description:
202 response body for POST /ingest/upload (ADR-0020 §2.1, M4-EXT non-blocking).

file_path:  saved path relative to vault_root (e.g. "raw/sources/notes.md")
status:     always "queued" — the watcher picks up the file asynchronously.
overwritten: true if a same-name file already existed and was replaced on disk.

page_id is not returned because ingest is async (watcher-driven); poll GET /ingest/runs
or GET /pages to confirm the page exists after ingest completes (~15-30s).

VaultMetaWriteRequest.title: VaultMetaWriteRequest
VaultMetaWriteRequest.description: Request body for PUT /vault/meta/{name} — the full new file content.

display_name.title: Display Name
display_name.description: Human-readable vendor name for the UI.

provider_type.title: Provider Type
provider_type.description: Backend family: api | local | cli (maps to I6 routing).

default_base_url.title: Default Base Url
default_base_url.description: OpenAI-compatible endpoint the ApiProvider keys off. null = Anthropic-native (anthropic), no HTTP endpoint (local/cli), or must be set per deployment (azure-openai). The user can always override in the row.

needs_api_key.title: Needs Api Key
needs_api_key.description: True iff this vendor requires an API key (api vendors); false for local/cli.

model_presets.title: Model Presets
model_presets.description: Known model ids for the dropdown; the user may type a custom id.

notes.title: Notes
notes.description: Short UI help text for the vendor row.

VendorInfo.title: VendorInfo
VendorInfo.description: One vendor row in the Settings catalog (W1).

set_url.title: Set Url
set_url.description: Set the SearXNG base URL in vault_state (DB wins over env). Must be a valid http(s) URL. Set to null to clear the DB URL (falls back to SEARXNG_URL env).

set_categories.title: Set Categories
set_categories.description: Comma-separated SearXNG categories (e.g. 'general,news'). Empty string "" clears to default. Omit to leave unchanged.

set_max_queries.title: Set Max Queries
set_max_queries.description: Max SearXNG queries per deep-research iteration (1–50). Omit to leave unchanged.

clear.title: Clear
clear.description: If true, clear ALL DB overrides (url, categories, max_queries). After clear, all three fall back to env / code defaults. Applied FIRST; then set_* fields are applied.

WebSearchConfigRequest.title: WebSearchConfigRequest
WebSearchConfigRequest.description:
Request body for PUT /web-search/config (ADR-0041 §2.4).

All fields are optional; omitting a field leaves that aspect unchanged.
No provider field — SearXNG is the ONLY web-search backend (I9).
Passing any non-SearXNG provider name is rejected with 422 (I9 guard).

configured.title: Configured
configured.description: True iff a SearXNG URL is available (DB or env). POST /research/start returns 503 when false.

url.title: Url
url.description: Resolved SearXNG base URL (DB wins over env; ADR-0041 §2.2). None when neither DB nor env is set. NOT a secret — returned in full (unlike clip/mcp tokens).

categories.title: Categories
categories.description: Resolved SearXNG categories list (DB wins over env/default; ADR-0041 §2.2). Empty list when neither DB nor env sets this — SearXNG uses its own default.

max_queries.title: Max Queries
max_queries.description: Resolved max SearXNG queries per deep-research iteration (DB wins over DEEP_RESEARCH_MAX_QUERIES env; ADR-0041 §2.2).

source.title: Source
source.description: "db" | "env" | "none" — which URL source is authoritative (ADR-0041 §2.2). "db": URL set via PUT /web-search/config. "env": SEARXNG_URL env var. "none": no URL configured.

WebSearchConfigResponse.title: WebSearchConfigResponse
WebSearchConfigResponse.description:
Response model for GET /web-search/config (ADR-0041 §2.3).

The SearXNG URL is NOT a secret — it IS returned (unlike the clip token).
source values: "db" | "env" | "none".

configured.title: Configured
configured.description: True iff a SearXNG URL is now available.

url.title: Url
url.description: Resolved SearXNG URL post-write (not a secret).

WebSearchConfigStateResponse.title: WebSearchConfigStateResponse
WebSearchConfigStateResponse.description:
Response body for PUT /web-search/config (ADR-0041 §2.4).

Always reflects post-write posture.

key.title: Key
key.description: API key to store; omit when clearing

clear.title: Clear
clear.description: True to remove the stored key (env resumes)

WebSearchProviderKeyRequest.title: WebSearchProviderKeyRequest
WebSearchProviderKeyRequest.description: PUT /web-search/provider-keys — set (key) or clear (clear=true) one provider's key.

configured.title: Configured
configured.description: True if a key is set (DB or env)

WebSearchProviderKeyState.title: WebSearchProviderKeyState
WebSearchProviderKeyState.description: Masked posture for one cloud web-search provider — NEVER the key value.

secrets_available.title: Secrets Available
secrets_available.description: True if SYNAPSE_SECRET_KEY is set (required to store keys via the UI)

WebSearchProviderKeysResponse.title: WebSearchProviderKeysResponse
WebSearchProviderKeysResponse.description: GET /web-search/provider-keys — masked posture for all cloud providers.

ErrorEnvelope.title: ErrorEnvelope
ErrorEnvelope.description: Stable error envelope for every error response (ADR-0086).

code.description: Stable, machine-readable snake_case error code (e.g. not_found, validation_error). Public contract.

details.description: Optional structured payload (field-level validation errors for 422s); null for simple errors.
